title: Heptapleurum samoense
taxonomy: Heptapleurum samoense is classified within the family Araliaceae. This plant is also known as the Samoan ivy or Samoan polynesian ivy. As a member of the family Araliaceae, it is related to other plants such as ivy and ginseng. The classification of Heptapleurum samoense is based on its physical characteristics and genetic makeup. Heptapleurum samoense is a species of flowering plant native to the Pacific Islands.
uses: Heptapleurum samoense is a plant species with reported medicinal properties, particularly in traditional medicine. It is said to have been used to treat various health conditions, including fever and skin infections.
